author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
The Catholic Faith Concerning Law, Grace, and Free Will.
title: The Catholic Faith Concerning Law, Grace, and Free Will.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: Now the Lord Himself not only shows us what evil we should shun, and what good we should do, which is all that the letter of the law is able to effect; but He moreover helps us that we may shun evil and do good, which none can do without the Spirit of grace; and if this be wanting, the law comes in merely to make us guilty and to slay us.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: It is on this account that the apostle says, “The letter killeth, but the Spirit giveth life.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: He, then, who lawfully uses the law learns therein evil and good, and, not trusting in his own strength, flees to grace, by the help of which he may shun evil and do good.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: But who is there who flees to grace except when “the steps of a man are ordered by the Lord, and He shall determine his way”?

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: And thus also to desire the help of grace is the beginning of grace; of which, says he, “And I said, Now I have begun; this is the change of the right hand of the Most High.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: It is to be confessed, therefore, that we have free choice to do both evil and good; but in doing evil every one is free from righteousness and a servant of sin, while in doing good no one can be free, unless he have been made free by Him who said, “If the Son shall make you free, then you shall be free indeed.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: Neither is it thus, that when any one has been made free from the dominion of sin, he no longer needs the help of his Deliverer; but rather thus, that hearing from Him, “Without me ye can do nothing,” he himself also says to Him, “Be thou my helper!

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: I rejoice that I have found in our brother Florus also this faith, which without doubt is the true and prophetical and apostolical and catholic faith; whence those are the rather to be corrected—whom indeed I now think to have been corrected by the favour of God—who did not understand him.
